user_message: Give me the at-risk watchlist for my team. But honestly, half these accounts have been on the list for weeks without changing -- I only want to see the ones where something actually shifted since Friday.

watchlist_current.risk_driver: Contract renewal risk - no engagement 60 days

usability_anchors: Score 5: Recognizes the manager's request to filter for delta-only and leads with changes (Proseware new entry, Lucerne confidence upgrade, Tailspin removed); still provides full list but clearly separates 'changed since Friday' from 'stable'; acknowledges that daily watchlist adds noise for stable accounts
Score 3: Produces standard watchlist with delta indicators but doesn't restructure output to prioritize the manager's specific ask; still shows all 6 accounts with equal weight
Score 1: Dumps full watchlist without any delta differentiation; ignores the manager's explicit request to focus on changes
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Each account's status correctly reflects the data (days on watchlist, recovery actions in flight, delta indicators); Tailspin removal is noted
Score 3: Most data correct but misses one delta or incorrectly marks a stable account as changed
Score 1: Delta indicators are wrong or absent
decision_anchors: Score 5: For the assumption-stress dimension -- acknowledges that daily cadence creates noise for stable accounts (FM-WF09-K); suggests the stable accounts (Fourth Coffee at 42 days, Margie's at 56 days, GDI at 28 days) may benefit from weekly-only surfacing rather than daily repetition
Score 3: Presents all accounts but doesn't address the underlying assumption-stress about daily cadence value
Score 1: Treats all accounts identically regardless of time-on-list or stability

guardrail_checks: This is an assumption-stress scenario testing FM-WF09-K: daily cadence wastes value for stable accounts
The orchestrator should serve the user's expressed need (delta focus) while maintaining watchlist completeness
Guardrail axis scored neutrally per assumption-stress scoring mode
